project_overview.project_name: Nigeria Accelerating Resource Mobilization Reforms (ARMOR) Program for Results
project_overview.project_development_objective: To raise non-oil revenues and safeguard oil and gas revenues
project_overview.total_financing: US$750 million (IBRD) + US$423.5 million (Government counterpart) = US$1,173.5 million total
project_overview.beneficiaries: Federal Government of Nigeria, Federal Inland Revenue Service (FIRS), Nigeria Customs Service (NCS), Federal Ministry of Finance (FMF), taxpayers, traders, and citizens through improved public services funded by increased revenues

geographic_scope.fragile_or_conflict_affected_areas: yes - Nigeria is classified as a fragile state with ongoing security challenges including insecurity affecting oil production and economic activity

key_components.component_name: Result Area 1: Implement Tax and Excise Reforms
key_components.description: Support VAT regime reform, rationalize tax expenditures, and increase revenues from pro-health and green taxes (tobacco, alcohol, online betting, vehicles, plastics)

key_components.component_name: Result Area 2: Strengthen Tax Revenue and Customs Administrations
key_components.description: Enhance voluntary compliance through e-filing/e-payment, implement e-invoicing for VAT, improve tax audits through centralized risk-based systems, facilitate compliant trade flows, and strengthen post-clearance audits

key_components.component_name: Result Area 3: Safeguard Oil and Gas Revenues
key_components.description: Conduct forensic audit of NNPCL, enhance NNPCL reporting to FAAC with detailed information on oil lifted, liabilities, forward sales, and fiscal payments
Technical assistance to FIRS and NCS, support for PCU capacity building, independent verification agency procurement, communications strategy, M&E systems, and e-waste management

implementation_arrangements.implementing_agency: Federal Ministry of Finance (FMF) with Federal Inland Revenue Service (FIRS) and Nigeria Customs Service (NCS) as co-implementing agencies
implementation_arrangements.coordination_mechanisms: Three-tier governance structure: (1) ARMOR Program Steering Committee chaired by Federal Minister of Finance meeting quarterly; (2) ARMOR Technical Committee chaired by Permanent Secretary of Finance meeting monthly; (3) Project Coordinating Unit (PCU) housed in Technical Services Directorate reporting to PSF, meeting weekly. FIRS and NCS have their own Program Implementing Units (PIUs)
implementation_arrangements.procurement_approach: Nigeria Public Procurement Act (2007) governs procurement. World Bank Standard Procurement Documents for International Competitive Bidding. National Standard Bidding Documents for NCB with World Bank Anti-Corruption Guidelines applied. Major procurement: Independent Verification Agent (IVA) engagement
implementation_arrangements.monitoring_and_evaluation: PCU responsible for M&E with dedicated M&E expert. Monitoring of DLIs/DLRs through verification protocols. IVA hired to verify achievement of DLRs. Mid-year and annual progress reports submitted to World Bank. World Bank conducts implementation missions, mid-term review, and implementation completion report

identified_risks.risk_description: High risk from currency devaluation and fuel subsidy removal causing inflation and rising cost of living, potentially triggering protests. Significant overlaps in institutional roles and responsibilities make coordinated efforts challenging. Perceptions of corruption and weak institutions dampen business confidence
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: High-level government buy-in from new administration committed to reforms. Government rolled out social protection program to mitigate adverse impacts on poor and vulnerable. Communications strategy to be developed and implemented. TA support for stakeholder engagement and awareness

identified_risks.risk_description: Oil revenues susceptible to global oil price volatility. External uncertainties (Russia-Ukraine conflict, global monetary tightening) could reduce capital flows and increase sovereign risk. Failure to contain inflation and currency depreciation could undermine macroeconomic stability
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: ARMOR Program designed to raise tax revenues and safeguard oil revenues to mitigate impact of oil price changes. Complementary US$1.5 billion RESET-DPF supports strong macroeconomic policies to control inflation and maintain appropriate exchange rate. FRED 2.0 programmatic ASA provides TA on tax policy implementation

identified_risks.risk_description: High risk from limited knowledge and experience in NCS and FIRS on World Bank PforR operations and procurement rules. Risk of program funds not used for intended purposes. Backlog of unaudited financial statements (FY22 in NCS, FY21-22 in FMF). Inadequate internal audit function in FMF
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Training and capacity building interventions financed through IPF component. Bi-annual reporting on use of funds using pre-existing systems. Annual audits and verification by IVA. World Bank implementation missions to verify results. Risk-based internal audit implementation. Dedicated procurement and FM staff. Submission of interim and program financial reports based on PEF

identified_risks.risk_category: Institutional Capacity for Implementation and Sustainability
identified_risks.risk_description: While coordination mechanisms are clear, challenges may arise in receiving cooperation from NNPCL affecting Program implementation. Independent nature of participating agencies may constrain achievement of ambitious results such as integrating operational systems
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Annual work planning process and governance structures led by FMF provide platform to anticipate and mobilize advisory support. Targeted TA supported by IPF. Many actions follow regulatory changes supported through RESET-DPF. FIRS and NCS budgets deducted at source ensuring continuing funding. Both agencies have training schools for continuous staff development

identified_risks.risk_description: Substantial risk of opposition from vested industry groups to tax increases. Labor unions and civil society may oppose policies perceived as hurting vulnerable populations. Potential social unrest from tax rate increases
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Poverty and social incidence analysis concluded measures largely borne by rich deciles. Consultations conducted during ESSA to raise awareness of importance of revenue mobilization. Green tax exempts vehicles under 2000cc as poor rely on motorbikes. Stakeholder engagement and communication plan implementation. Grievance Redress Mechanism to process complaints

identified_risks.risk_category: Technical Design of Project or Program
identified_risks.risk_description: Limited success of previous initiatives to raise revenues. Tax policy and revenue administration reforms are inherently challenging. Vested stakeholder interests and lobbying can hinder implementation
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Program design anchored in sound analytical work (PEFA 2019, TADAT 2023, IMF lessons learned). Government ARMOR program endorsed by Federal Executive Council. Extensive consultation with new administration on design. Communications strategy to be developed and implemented

identified_risks.risk_description: Environmental risk rated Low - no construction or civil works. Social risk rated Substantial from tax rate increases affecting certain industries and populations. Potential e-waste generation from increased ICT use. Risk of social unrest from tax increases
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: E-waste strategy to be developed and implemented by FIRS and NCS. Stakeholder engagement and communication plan. Green taxes designed to be progressive (exempting small vehicles, mass transit). Pro-health taxes largely affect rich deciles. Grievance Redress Mechanism. Dedicated E&S officials in PCU

identified_risks.risk_description: Risk of limited success of revenue reforms due to implementation challenges and resistance to change
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Program anchored in government priorities and pronouncements. Federal Executive Council endorsement. Extensive stakeholder consultation. Technical assistance support throughout implementation

safeguards_and_social.environmental_category: Low (for IPF component) - no construction, rehabilitation, or data center investments
triggered_safeguard_policies: ESS 1: Assessment and Management of Environmental and Social Risks and Impacts (Relevant)
ESS 10: Stakeholder Engagement and Information Disclosure (Relevant)
ESS 3: Resource Efficiency and Pollution Prevention and Management (Relevant)
safeguards_and_social.grievance_redress_mechanism: yes - Multiple mechanisms: (1) Program-specific GRM through implementing agencies (FIRS, NCS, FMF) to process complaints from taxpayers and traders; (2) Modified taxpayers complaints redressal system to be prepared within 6 months of effectiveness; (3) World Bank Grievance Redress Service (GRS) for affected communities and individuals; (4) World Bank Inspection Panel for complaints of Bank non-compliance with policies
safeguards_and_social.gender_considerations: Program prioritizes gender equity in tax administration and policy. Actions include: (1) Conducting gender impact analysis on revenue policy proposals; (2) Collecting and disseminating gender-disaggregated data on business ownership and leadership; (3) Enhancing online filing to reduce physical interfaces between FIRS and female taxpayers, reducing harassment risk; (4) Establishing grievance mechanisms for women to file complaints related to harassment and corruption; (5) Taxpayer outreach to increase voluntary compliance and build trust, particularly among women traders
vulnerable_groups: Poor and most vulnerable populations - mitigated through poverty and social incidence analysis showing measures largely borne by rich deciles
Small-scale traders and informal sector workers - supported through simplified tax administration and e-filing to reduce compliance costs
Women-owned businesses - supported through reduced physical tax office visits via e-filing, reduced harassment risk, and gender-disaggregated data collection
Low-income vehicle owners - protected through exemption of vehicles under 2000cc from green excise
Public transport users - protected through exemption of mass-transit vehicles from green excise

fcv_relevant_details.conflict_sensitivity: Program addresses conflict-related fiscal vulnerabilities by: (1) Reducing dependence on oil revenues vulnerable to production disruptions from insecurity; (2) Strengthening domestic revenue mobilization to reduce fiscal vulnerability to external shocks; (3) Improving transparency in oil sector governance to reduce corruption and build trust in state institutions; (4) Supporting social protection spending through increased revenues to help mitigate grievances; (5) Enhancing tax administration efficiency to improve service delivery and state legitimacy
fcv_relevant_details.fragility_context: Nigeria classified as fragile state with key fragility factors: (1) High dependence on oil revenues (1.8% of GDP in 2023) vulnerable to price volatility and production disruptions from insecurity; (2) Low non-oil tax revenues (3.7% of GDP in 2023) limiting fiscal space for development; (3) Weak institutions and governance challenges including perceptions of corruption dampening business confidence; (4) Limited transparency and accountability in oil sector governance; (5) Insecurity affecting oil production (fell from 1.8 mbpd in 2020 to 1.4 mbpd in 2022-23); (6) Weak state capacity and service delivery; (7) High poverty (over 40% below poverty line) and inequality
fcv_relevant_details.security_considerations: Program acknowledges security challenges: (1) Insecurity cited as factor reducing oil production and economic activity; (2) Program design focuses on federal level implementation where security is more stable; (3) No direct security sector financing; (4) Improved revenues could support security spending and social protection to address grievances; (5) Enhanced governance and transparency could build trust in state institutions and reduce conflict drivers
fcv_relevant_details.displacement_or_refugees: no - Program does not involve land acquisition, resettlement, or displacement. No direct impact on refugee or IDP populations. However, improved fiscal space from increased revenues could support humanitarian assistance and social protection for displaced populations
